hypothesis: Incorporating explicit chiral annotations in the SMILES encoder and using enantiomeric pairs as hard negatives during contrastive pretraining will yield embeddings that achieve at least 90% accuracy on enantiomer discrimination in a held-out chiral benchmark while maintaining SO(3) pose invariance with unseen-rotation retrieval Accuracy@10 within 2 percentage points of the original SMILESDFT-CLIP model.
